Yatharth Hospital Q4FY26 Concall Highlights 

🔶️ FY27+ Management Guidance

• Growth
  >> FY27 revenue expected to surpass FY26's 36% YoY rate 🚀
• Margins
  >> Reaffirming 24%–25% EBITDA margin, with upside as new assets mature
• ARPOB & Mix
  >> Targeting ~10% annual ARPOB growth
  >> Gurugram modeled to launch at ₹50K+
  >> Aiming to reduce government scheme share from 35% to 25% over two years 🚀
• Target
  >> Reach 5,000 beds within 3 years via internal accruals (70% acquisitions) 🚀

🔶️ Key Highlights

 • Tariff Boost
  >> Dec'25 CGHS rate hikes added ~5% to revenue and ~3% to EBITDA
 • ARPOB
  >> Group average grew 7% YoY to ₹33,124
  >> Noida Extension peaked at ₹47.8K
 • Occupancy
  >> 71% in Q4 (68% for FY26)
  >> Noida & Jhansi topped at 86%
 • Oncology
  >> Now 10% of group revenue, peaking at ~30% share in Noida Extension

🔶️ Expansions & Acquisitions

• Gurugram (New): ₹200 Cr total investment & opens in April 2027
• Agra (Acquired Feb'26): Already profitable with ₹7 Cr/month run-rate & 18% EBITDA
• New Delhi & Faridabad (Sec 20): Contributed 11% of Q4 revenue & initial ARPOBs tracking strong at ~₹40K and ~₹38K
• Capacity
  >> Currently at 3,200+ beds
  >> Brownfield expansions active at Greater Noida & Noida Extension (₹75L/bed capex)
• Partnership: Secured exclusive hospital rights for the upcoming Noida International Airport (Jewar)

reports.text:
Bharti Airtel Management on ARPU

Gopal Vittal’s clearly said that the price architecture in India is broken

Management compared this with global markets:
India unlimited data - Rs 340-350
US unlimited data - $60-70
Europe unlimited data - €35

At Rs 340-350, customers are capped out with unlimited data plans

This means even if data consumption keeps rising, revenue per GB does not increase meaningfully

The key point is that India remains structurally under-monetised on data pricing

Management also made an important point that the rich are paying less than they should, while the poor are probably paying as much as they need to

ARPU stood at 257 in Q4, Management was clearly not satisfied with the Q4 ARPU movement

Larger point is that Airtel wants to accelerate ARPU growth from here

NSE TRADING HOURS CHANGE – EFFECTIVE 3 AUGUST 2026 📊

Key Change
- F&O market closes at 3:40 PM
- Earlier closing time was 3:30 PM

Why The Change?
- New cash market closing auction introduced
- Auction session from 3:15–3:35 PM
- Helps determine official closing price
- Improves price discovery process

Benefits For Traders
- Extra 10 minutes for hedging
- Better position adjustments possible
- Reduced uncertainty at market close
- Better alignment with cash market closing

Other Changes
- Close price VWAP window: 3:10–3:40 PM
- Trade modification till 4:15 PM
- Pre-open timing unchanged
- Market opening timing unchanged

Market Impact
- More efficient closing price discovery
- Better cash and F&O market alignment
- Improved execution near market close

FY27 may not be a blockbuster for Quality Power, especially the first half.
But once new capex goes live, growth could kick in a big way.

1. Sangli Global Coil Manufacturing Facility: Goes live by Dec 2026, Management guided ~INR 1,500 crores — roughly 6x the standalone FY26 revenue of ~INR 246 crores from this facility itself.

2. . High-Voltage Test Laboratory: Goes live by July- Aug 2026.

3. GIS Manufacturing & Testing Infrastructure: First GIS prototypes expected July–August 2026; additional oven capacity being added post-September 2026. Monetisation (revenue contribution) is expected to begin in roughly 12 months from now, i.e., mid-FY28.

4. New BESS/PCS Manufacturing Facility at Nigde(Turkey): Targeted for December 2026. The 1,725 KW PCS inverter launch is expected within 2 quarters i.e., by Q2/Q3 FY27

5. CTC Cable / Magnet Wire Facility: commissioning targeted by Q3 FY27

reports.text:
Indian Equity Market has witnessed massive activity from the FPIs on the day of MSCI rebalancing. 

Out of  total turnover of Rs 287,452 Crore on NSE , FPIs participation was Rs 198,465 Crore about ~ 69 %. 

For a Net sell of Rs 20,637 crore FPIs traded Rs 198,465 crore about ~ 9.6 X 

For a Net buy of Rs 16,260 Crore DIIs traded Rs 53,772 crore about  3.3 X. 

Massive participation by FPIs in a market which isn’t on their radar ? 
 
Is it MSCI rebalancing or HFT trades on MSCI rebalancing? 

Out of the net FPI outflow of Rs 20,637 crore how much will be HFTs ?

Via Nilesh Shah

Solid Set from Supriya🔥

Management reiterated its FY27 outlook:
FY27 REV target - 1,000 Cr (20% growth)

Guidance looks achievable because FY26 exit run rate is strong, new products have started contributing & several growth levers are still in early ramp-up phase

Entering FY27 with multiple growth drivers

Cardiovascular intermediate has started scaling well & management has already locked contracts with 3-4 large users

ADHD product is seeing strong demand from LATAM & Europe

Liquid anesthetic API has started seeing steady monthly supply

DSM contract has stabilised & can move closer to peak REV of around 60 Cr in FY27

Contrast media launch has been shifted to H2

3-4 new product launches will continue

Growth may not be linear across quarters due to annual maintenance shutdown in Aug’26

reports.text:
*Deepak Fertilizers Concall Highlights:*

* Q4FY26 remained challenging due to LPG/LNG shortages, higher raw material prices, delayed subsidy support, China’s export restrictions, and manpower shortages. However, management indicated that business conditions have started improving in Q1FY27.

* Propane shortages impacted the IPA business, while LNG supply disruptions affected fertilizer and chemical operations. The company has now started receiving LNG supplies under its 15-year contract with Equinor, improving supply security and cost visibility.

* The planned shutdown of the ammonia plant impacted Q4 profitability by ~₹70–75 crore. Post-upgrade, the plant is operating at improved efficiency and higher capacity utilization.

* Crop Nutrition margins remained under pressure due to elevated input costs and delayed subsidy revisions. Government-controlled fertilizer prices also restricted the company’s ability to fully pass on higher costs.

* Specialty and smart fertilizers continued to witness strong customer acceptance, with Specialty and Croptek contribution increasing to 33% of segment revenue.

* Management remains positive on the Mining Chemicals and TAN business. The Gopalpur TAN facility is expected to provide strong freight advantages, stable ammonia availability, and supply reliability.

* FY26 consolidated revenue grew 12% YoY to ₹11,506 crore, while Q4 revenue stood at ₹3,011 crore, driven by strong volume growth in mining chemicals and crop nutrition.

* FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹1,684 crore and PAT at ₹739 crore. Profitability was impacted by higher fertilizer input costs and weak IPA spreads during the second half.

* Mining Chemicals volumes grew 11% YoY in FY26, while the B2C segment contribution increased to 16% from 13% last year, improving realizations and customer engagement.

* Industrial Chemicals performance remained mixed due to IPA-related challenges, though management highlighted early recovery signs supported by tighter global supply conditions and lower imports.

* FY26 capex stood at ₹1,569 crore, while net debt stood at ₹4,824 crore with Net Debt/EBITDA at 2.86x.

* The Gopalpur TAN project is 95% complete and the Dahej Nitric Acid project is 86% complete, with commissioning expected in Q2FY27. Management expects Gopalpur utilization to reach 90–95% by the end of FY27.

* Total project capex guidance remains around ₹4,650 crore, of which ~₹3,800 crore has already been spent. The company expects pending capex of ₹800–1,000 crore in FY27, after which capex should normalize to maintenance levels.

* Management highlighted strong ammonia supply visibility, supported by in-house production of over 500,000 tonnes along with long-term supplier contracts and import capabilities.

* Management indicated that ammonia margins remain favorable due to elevated ammonia prices and Henry Hub-linked gas contracts, which are expected to support strong profitability in the ammonia business.

* Going forward, management expects improvement in both growth and margins, driven by higher realizations, new capacity additions at Gopalpur and other facilities, stable LNG sourcing, and operating leverage benefits.

TRANSWORLD SHIPPING SELLS VESSEL FOR $5.7 MILLION 🚢💰

• 🤝 Transworld Shipping Lines has signed an MoA (Memorandum of Agreement) with Avana Logistek Ltd

• 🚢 Agreement pertains to the sale of vessel M.V. SSL Thamirabarani

• 💵 Deal value stands at US$ 5.7 Million (around ₹49 Crore)

• ⚓ Vessel owned by Transworld Shipping (formerly Shreyas Shipping & Logistics)

• 📅 Delivery to be completed within one day prior to delivery date as per agreement terms

• ✅ Transaction is not a related-party deal

• 📑 Company disclosed the agreement under SEBI regulations

🟢 Impact: Positive (Asset monetization strengthens liquidity and capital allocation flexibility, while proceeds can support debt reduction, fleet optimization or future growth initiatives.)

reports.text:
POWERGRID WINS NEW KARNATAKA TRANSMISSION PROJECT ⚡🏗️

• 🏆 POWERGRID declared the successful bidder under TBCB route for Tumkur II RE Transmission Ltd

• 🌞 Project aimed at integration of 7 GW Renewable Energy potential at Tumkur-II, Karnataka

• 🔌 Project includes:

400 kV D/c Transmission Line

ICT augmentation works

Additional Line Bays at Tumkur-II Pooling Station

• 🏗️ To be implemented on BOOT (Build, Own, Operate & Transfer) basis

• 💰 Acquired the SPV for an aggregate consideration of approximately ₹15.46 Crore

• 📊 POWERGRID acquired 100% shareholding of the project SPV

• 🌱 Strengthens POWERGRID's renewable energy transmission portfolio and supports India's clean energy expansion

• 🤝 Project awarded through Tariff-Based Competitive Bidding (TBCB) process coordinated by PFCCL

🟢 Impact: Positive (Adds to POWERGRID's transmission project pipeline, strengthens renewable energy integration capabilities and supports long-term regulated asset growth.)

📍 ORDER WIN

SOLAR INDUSTRIES INDIA: Secures ₹1,076 Cr defence export order from international clients for supply of defence products with 3-year execution period.

SIMPLEX CASTINGS: Secures key orders from ThyssenKrupp, BHEL and SMS India strengthening high-precision industrial castings business.

MAGELLANIC CLOUD: Subsidiary wins ₹7.64 Cr South Central Railway CCTV surveillance order with 5-year CAMC support.

SHARIKA ENTERPRISES: Bags ₹24.9 Cr smart grid order under Uttarakhand RDSS scheme for SCADA and automation systems.

UNIVASTU INDIA: Receives ₹115.79 Cr variation order from IRCON for Mumbai Metro Line 6 electrical and E&M systems.

📍 FUNDRAISING

HIMATSINGKA SEIDE: Board approves raising up to ₹850 Cr via NCDs mainly for refinancing debt.

📍 ACQUISITION

UNIMECH AEROSPACE: Acquires Hobel Bellows strengthening engineering capabilities and product portfolio.

LUPIN: Philippines acquisition timeline extended to July 2026; deal remains on track with no change in structure.

📍 COLLABORATION

ORIENTAL RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE: Partners with HUM International for smart wagon technology and collaborates with United Wagon for next-gen wagons.

📍 FII STAKE CHANGES / BOUGHT-SOLD

MSCI REBALANCING: India expected to see net outflows; Federal Bank, MCX, NALCO, Indian Bank added while RVNL, Kalyan Jewellers, Jubilant FoodWorks, Hyundai Motor India excluded.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

ASIAN PAINTS: Q4 sales up 10.8% YoY with margin expansion driven by volume growth and cost efficiency.

INDIGO: Reports sharp PAT loss in Q4 due to forex impact despite operational strength and capacity growth.

HIMATSINGKA SEIDE: FY27 recovery outlook with India focus, new product verticals and FTAs supporting growth.

BANK OF MAHARASHTRA: Revises MCLR rates with overnight rate cut and mixed adjustments across tenors.

RATEGAIN: Releases APMEA report highlighting AI-driven disruption in hotel bookings and digital shift risks.

MRS. BECTOR’S FOOD SPECIALITIES: Expands manufacturing footprint with new bakery and biscuit capacities across India.

ORIENTAL RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE: FY26 profit surge driven by smart wagons, leasing business and strong order book.

SIMPLEX CASTINGS: Revenue, EBITDA and PAT all grow strongly with railway approvals and debt reduction.

GAUDIUM IVF: Transition to listed scalable IVF platform with AI adoption and expansion into 19 hubs via IPO proceeds.

UNIMECH AEROSPACE: Strong global expansion, nuclear orders and ₹314 Cr order book supporting long-term growth.

ECORECO: Highlights long-term opportunity in critical minerals and battery recycling; no confirmed contract yet.

RIR POWER: Advances semiconductor plant and secures first overseas SCR thyristor order.

E2E NETWORKS: Deploys NVIDIA B200 AI cluster strengthening sovereign AI cloud capabilities.

HIMATSINGKA SEIDE: FY26 revenue growth supported by forex gains, capacity utilization challenges persist.

📍 CREDIT RATINGS

BANK OF MAHARASHTRA: Minor mixed MCLR adjustments; overall lending rate impact remains limited.

📍 LAUNCHES

GAUDIUM IVF: Becomes first IVF chain in India using AI-led embryology platforms improving success rates.

E2E NETWORKS: Launches NVIDIA B200-based AI infrastructure for enterprise-grade AI workloads.

RATEGAIN: Introduces AI-focused report on hotel booking disruption and digital transformation trends.

📍 IT NEWS

TCS: Denies reports of RBC Canada mandate loss, calls claims false and unverified.

📍 OTHERS

ZYDUS LIFESCIENCES: Raises buyback price to ₹1,260 per share while reducing share quantity.

MSCI REBALANCING: Expected India net outflows due to index changes and float-factor adjustments.

BRENT CRUDE: Falls below $93 on ceasefire hopes and easing geopolitical risk; positive for oil importers.

GOVT POLICY: May consider LTCG tax relief and bond tax incentives to attract foreign inflows and support rupee.

VIRTUOSO OPTOELECTRONICS: Receives BSE Mainboard migration approval from SME platform.

Ashok Leyland: Guidance - Concall Update

Management expressed cautious optimism for FY27, anticipating positive demand drivers for commercial vehicles but acknowledging macroeconomic headwinds like global economic uncertainties, commodity price volatility, and diesel price increases.

FY27 capital expenditure is projected to be between INR750 crores and INR1,000 crores.

Investments in subsidiaries will be need-based, with Hinduja Leyland Finance, Hinduja Housing Finance, and OHM Mobility potentially requiring funds for growth plans, while Switch Mobility India is now profitable and in a comfortable position.

The Defense business is expected to continue its 20% growth trend for the next two to three years, supported by an order book exceeding INR1,500 crores, though execution spans multiple years.

Non-vehicle revenue momentum is expected to continue into Q1 FY27.

Q1 FY27 industry-level commercial vehicle performance is anticipated to be better than Q1 last year, driven by resilient baseline demand, fleet replacement, and GST benefits, despite current macroeconomic challenges.

For Q1 FY27, significant commodity cost increases, particularly in steel, pose a challenge to margins, which the company plans to mitigate through a 1-1.5% price increase and cost control measures, though sustaining the price increase for the full quarter is uncertain.

New product launches are expected to positively impact market share in the tipper and tractor trailer segments starting Q2 FY27.

Management anticipates some moderation in LCV and ICV demand from Q4 FY26 levels, but a relative pickup in heavy-duty trucks, which is favorable for the product mix.

**ALKEM LABORATORIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹6,890

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue was in line with estimates
- EBITDA beat estimates by 11%
- Gross margin improved to 65.4%
- Gross margin expanded by 606 bps YoY

**Exceptional Items**
- Exceptional loss of ₹1.35 Bn reported
- ₹747 Mn impairment charge on real estate investments
- ₹603 Mn impact from changes in the Labor Code

**PAT Performance**
- PAT impacted by exceptional items
- Higher effective tax rate also affected profitability
- Exceptional charges are one-off in nature

**FY27 Outlook**
- FY27 guidance was in line with or ahead of current expectations

**Valuation**
- Trades at 23.6x FY27F adjusted EPS
- Trades at 19.7x FY28F adjusted EPS
- FY27F adjusted EPS estimated at ₹230.8
- FY28F adjusted EPS estimated at ₹276.2

**APAR INDUSTRIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price raised to ₹14,240

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Strong Q4FY26 performance
- EBITDA beat Nomura estimates by 3%
- EBITDA beat consensus estimates by 5%

**Conductor Business**
- Conductor EBITDA/MT grew 8% YoY in Q4FY26
- Demand remains structurally healthy

**Cable Business**
- Robust growth continues in cables
- DC cables offer premium pricing opportunities
- Strong demand outlook for DC cable segment

**Capex Plans**
- FY27 capex guidance at ₹15 Bn
- Investments aimed at supporting future growth

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27F EBITDA estimate raised by 7%
- FY28F EBITDA estimate raised by 9%
- EBITDA CAGR of 19% expected over FY26–FY29F

**Valuation**
- Trades at 37x FY27F EPS
- Trades at 29x FY28F EPS

**Investment View**
- Outlook remains on firm footing
- Strong demand across key business segments supports growth visibility

**VINATI ORGANICS – KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price: ₹1,200

**FY26 Performance**
- Revenue declined 1% in FY26
- Decline driven by lower revenues from IBB and ATBS

**Management Guidance**
- Management continues to guide for 15% volume growth in FY27

**Business Challenges**
- VOPL projects continue to struggle
- Market conditions remain a risk
- Competitive intensity continues to be a concern

**Earnings Outlook**
- Kotak reduced EPS estimates by a further 9–10%

**CUMMINS INDIA – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Downgraded to Neutral
- Target price: ₹6,000
- Stock considered fully priced in

**Growth Outlook**
- Demand drivers remain intact
- PAT CAGR of 18% expected over FY26–FY29F
- Domestic demand outlook remains constructive

**Q4 Performance**
- EBITDA beat estimates by 7%–9%
- Operating leverage offset gross margin contraction

**Margin Outlook**
- Near-term gross margin headwinds expected
- Commodity inflation may outpace pricing actions
- Margin pressure likely in the short term

**Valuation**
- Stock trades at 52x FY28F EPS
- Limited upside at current valuations

**ONGC – CLSA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- High Conviction Outperform
- Target price: ₹405

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- 4Q standalone PAT missed estimates by 22%
- Post-results analyst call revealed several one-off charges
- Adjusted core EBIT was in line with estimates
- Adjusted PBT was also in line with estimates

**Production Outlook**
- Management did not provide specific near-term guidance on production ramp-up
- Remains confident about new field development
- New fields expected to add more than 30% to gas production over the next two years
- Production additions expected to start from next quarter

**Mumbai High**
- Service contract with British Petroleum (BP) expected to provide support
- Likely to temporarily halt production decline at Mumbai High

**Valuation**
- Stock currently pricing in Brent crude at US$62/bbl
- CLSA sees over 44% upside using US$82/bbl Brent assumption for FY28
- Upside increases to 73% at current spot Brent of US$95/bbl

**BDL – GS REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price cut to ₹1,260

**Execution Concerns**
- Execution remains a key concern
- Company missed expected execution for the seventh consecutive year

**Reasons for FY27 Miss**
- Domestic supply chain issues
- Unavailability of a component from an overseas supplier

**Revenue Performance**
- FY26 revenue declined 27% YoY
- Underperformed compared to other defence peers under coverage

**Inventory Build-Up**
- Inventory surged 75% YoY to ₹46.3 Bn
- Inventory exceeds 2x FY26 revenue
- Increase mainly due to finished goods awaiting delivery

**Order Book Strength**
- FY26 order book estimated at ₹257 Bn
- Implies >10x book-to-bill ratio
- Highest among companies under coverage

**Order Inflow Outlook**
- FY27 order inflow expected at ₹140 Bn
- Order pipeline remains strong

**Key Monitorable**
- Pick-up in execution will be the key factor to watch

**MCX – UBS REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Downgraded to Neutral
- Target price raised to ₹3,600 from ₹3,200

**Earnings Outlook**
- Peak earnings momentum likely behind

**Volume Trends**
- Elevated volatility across key commodities in recent months
- Healthy Q1FY27-to-date volumes
- Volumes have normalized from Q4 levels

**Competition**
- Not worried about competition as of now

**Valuation**
- Stock trading at ~50x one-year forward P/E
- Around 10% above historical average valuation

**Investment View**
- Strong volume run-rate already priced in
- Limited upside seen from current levels

**BROKERAGE HIGHLIGHTS: KEY INITIATIONS & TARGET CHANGES**

**High Upside Calls**
- Axis Securities sees 39% upside in Camlin Fine.
- Systematix sees 51% upside in Krsnaa Diagnostics.
- Systematix sees 50% upside in Orchid Pharma.
- Axis Capital sees 61% upside in Pine Labs.
- PL Capital sees 36% upside in IRCTC.

**Infrastructure & Industrial Plays**
- Motilal Oswal sees 26% upside in KEC.
- Motilal Oswal sees 23% upside in Syrma SGS.
- Motilal Oswal sees 13% upside in Astra Microwave.
- Motilal Oswal sees 31% upside in Zensar.

**Consumer & Retail**
- Anand Rathi initiated BUY on Cello World.
- Emkay initiated BUY on SJS Enterprises.
- Motilal positive on ABFRL growth momentum.
- Sharekhan downgraded ABFRL to Hold.

**Healthcare & Pharma**
- Axis positive on Jubilant Ingrevia CDMO growth.
- PL Capital cautious on Jubilant Ingrevia valuations.

**Real Estate & Financials**

**Final Takeaway**
- Brokerages remain selectively bullish on manufacturing, healthcare, infrastructure, electronics and energy transition themes.

**SATIN CREDITCARE NETWORK – STRONG RECOVERY & RETURN RATIO IMPROVEMENT**  

**Brokerage View**
- Mirae Asset Sharekhan maintained POSITIVE view
- Target price revised to ₹260
- Implies 16% upside from CMP ₹223
- Stock trades at attractive 0.6x FY28E book value

**AUM Growth Momentum**
- Consolidated AUM grew 18.7% YoY to ₹15,174 Cr
- Disbursements surged 42.8% YoY
- MSME business grew 59.4% YoY
- Housing finance business increased 38% YoY

**Profitability Recovery**
- Net profit jumped to ₹162 Cr from ₹22 Cr YoY
- PPOP surged 126.7% YoY to ₹290 Cr
- Strong growth supported by lower opex and better asset quality
- RoA improved sharply to 4.27%

**Asset Quality Improvement**
- GNPA declined 60 bps YoY to 3.10%
- NNPA reduced 140 bps YoY to 0.90%
- Credit cost improved to 2.06%
- Collection efficiencies improved across buckets

**Margin & Income Trends**
- NIM declined to 6.6% due to higher foreign borrowing costs
- Other income surged to ₹291 Cr
- Opex/AUM improved by 60 bps to 6.6%
- Stable NIM guidance of 12–13% going forward

**Subsidiary Growth**
- Satin Finserv AUM grew 92.5% YoY
- Satin Housing Finance reached ₹1,267 Cr AUM
- Housing finance business delivering strong traction
- Diversification beyond microfinance strengthening

**FY27 Guidance**
- Consolidated AUM growth guided at 25–30%
- Standalone AUM expected at ₹14,800–15,100 Cr
- Credit cost guided at 3–3.5%
- RoA expected to improve above 3%

**Long-Term Targets**
- Consolidated AUM target revised to ₹32,000 Cr by 2030
- Non-MFI business targeted at 30% of total AUM
- FY26–FY28 PAT CAGR estimated at 36%
- Average RoE expected around 16.7% by FY28

**Valuation Snapshot**
- FY28E PAT estimated at ₹614 Cr
- FY28E EPS estimated at ₹55.8
- FY28E RoA projected at 3.1%
- FY28E RoE projected at 16.7%

**Key Risks**
- Forex exposure from global borrowings
- Regulatory risks in microfinance segment
- Rupee depreciation impacting borrowing costs
- Sector-wide asset quality volatility

Source: Mirae Asset Sharekhan

**YES SECURITIES – US EARNINGS & MARKET UPDATE**  

**US Market Outlook**
- S&P 500 earnings momentum remains exceptionally resilient
- CY2026 EPS estimates upgraded from USD 296 to USD 339 over the past year
- CY2026 earnings revision trend at +10%, second-best upgrade cycle in 8 years
- Earnings fundamentals continue validating market rally beyond valuation expansion

**Mega-Cap Technology Trends**
- Magnificent 7 continue leading US earnings growth
- Aggregate revenue growth for mega caps at ~30% versus mid-single digits for broader market
- Nvidia remains standout performer with triple-digit revenue and EBIT growth
- Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft continue posting superior profitability trends
- CY27 EPS growth for Magnificent 7 expected to moderate toward ~15%, converging with broader S&P 500 growth

**Sectoral Earnings Rotation**
- Industrials, Technology and Energy witnessing strongest earnings upgrades for CY2027
- Positive revisions seen in Dow, Intel, Seagate, Chevron and Devon Energy
- Signals improving cyclical recovery and capex-led expansion
- Energy and IT sectors show highest proportion of earnings upgrades

**Weakness & Downgrades**
- Consumer Discretionary and Communication Services witnessing maximum downgrade pressure
- Rate-sensitive and consumer-facing businesses remain under pressure
- Real estate-linked companies, cruise operators and media businesses seeing estimate cuts

**Market Breadth & Strategy View**
- Market leadership still concentrated in mega-cap technology
- Earnings breadth gradually improving beyond AI-led rally
- Selective cyclicals appear attractive due to improving estimate momentum
- Industrials, Energy and semiconductor-linked businesses remain preferred themes

**Key Data Points**
- S&P 500 CY27 earnings upgrades:
  - Magnificent 7: +4.4%
  - S&P 500 overall: +3.1%
  - S&P 500 excluding Mag 7: +1.4%
- S&P 500 Mar’26 earnings growth stood at 13.0% YoY
- EBIT growth for Mar’26 quarter at 12.6% YoY
- Revenue growth improved steadily to 7.5% YoY in Mar’26

Source: YES Securities Report

**YATHARTH HOSPITAL – PREMIUMISATION & NCR EXPANSION UPDATE**  

**Core Strategy**
- Focus shifting from bed addition to premium NCR healthcare platform
- Strategy focused on higher ARPOB improvement

**Operational Performance**
- Existing hospitals grew ~29% YoY in Q4FY26
- Occupancy remained healthy at 71% despite ~1,000 bed addition in FY26
- Strong demand absorption across NCR

**Margin Improvement**
- Government mix targeted to reduce from 35% to 25%
- Lower government mix expected to improve margins
- Lower government mix expected to improve ARPOB
- Lower government mix expected to improve working capital

**Oncology Growth**
- Oncology emerging as key profitability driver
- Noida Extension oncology mix increased from 20% to 30%

**Expansion Strategy**
- Nearly 70% of expansion is brownfield/acquisition-led
- Brownfield expansion allows faster breakeven
- Brownfield expansion lowers execution risk

**ARPOB Improvement**
- Noida Extension ARPOB reached ₹47.8K
- ARPOB increased 23% YoY

**FY27 Outlook**
- FY27 growth guidance remains strong
- Capex expected to be funded through internal accruals
- No equity dilution expected

Magellanic Cloud Limited Posts Landmark FY26 Results with ₹700+ Cr Total Income, Alongside Strong Q4 Performance with ₹205.5 Cr Revenue and 8% EBITDA Growth

📌FY26 Consolidated Performance Highlights
- Revenue from Operations stood at ₹697.9 Cr, registering a growth of 16.9% YoY. 
- Total Income increased to ₹706.8 Cr, reflecting a growth of 17.5% YoY. 
- Total Expenditure stood at ₹559.0 Cr, up 21.9% YoY. 
- EBITDA stood at ₹224.3 Cr, registering a growth of 7.0% YoY. 
- EBITDA Margin stood at 32.1%, compared to 35.1% in FY25. 
- Depreciation expense stood at ₹50.8 Cr, reflecting a growth of 19.6% YoY. 
- Finance Cost stood at ₹25.7 Cr, registering a growth of 5.0% YoY. 
- Profit Before Tax (PBT) stood at ₹147.8 Cr, reflecting a growth of 3.6% YoY. 
- Profit After Tax (PAT) stood at ₹114.4 Cr, registering a growth of 11.4% YoY. 

📌Q4 FY26 Consolidated Performance Highlights
- Revenue from Operations stood at ₹205.5 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹156.3 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 25.4% QoQ. 
- Total Income increased to ₹211.6 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹159.2 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 28.2% QoQ. 
- Total Expenditure was ₹176.1 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹128.7 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 35.1% QoQ. 
- EBITDA stood at ₹58.1 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹48.0 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 8.1% QoQ. 
- EBITDA Margin stood at 28.3% in Q4 FY26 as compared to 30.7% in Q4 FY25. 
- Depreciation expense stood at ₹15.7 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹10.6 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 27.9% QoQ. Finance Cost stood at ₹6.9 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹7.0 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 1.8% QoQ. 
- Profit Before Tax (PBT) stood at ₹35.5 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹30.5 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 2.4% QoQ. 
- Profit After Tax (PAT) stood at ₹30.4 Cr in Q4 FY26 as compared to ₹22.6 Cr in Q4 FY25, while registering a growth of 6.1% QoQ.

Anondita Medicare

Credit Rating Agencies - Brickwork Ratings Update:

1. Brand: Company has shifted its focus towards its proprietary brand “Cobra,” along with stable revenue from Government of India (CMSS) contracts.

2. New Markets: Company strategic expansion into international markets, including a five-year contract with the Government of South Africa. Further, its in-house manufacturing capabilities provide a cost advantage and support scalability.

3. Capacity Expansion: Company’s growth prospects remain dependent on timely completion and stabilization of the ongoing 16-line capacity expansion. 

4. Working capital: The business continues to be working capital intensive due to elongated credit cycles associated with government and export orders.

🚨 **MAX INDIA GETS MAJOR BOOST FROM ANTARA NOIDA OC APPROVAL**

MAX INDIA’s subsidiary Antara Senior Care has received Occupancy Certificate (OC) for Phase 1 of its Noida Sector 150 project, allowing possession of 340 senior living units.

**Why is this a big positive for MAX INDIA? 👇
**
💰 Unlocks nearly ₹150 Cr receivables, improving cash flow visibility

🏢 Phase 1 project value stands at ~₹550 Cr, supporting revenue recognition ahead

🚀 Opens growth pathway for Phase 2 project with expected revenue potential of ~₹800 Cr

👨‍🦳 Strengthens MAX INDIA’s positioning in India’s rapidly growing senior care & retirement housing sector

✅ Monetisation begins
✅ Cash flow visibility improves
✅ Execution confidence increases
✅ Long-term business potential strengthens

PRESTIGE ESTATES PROJECTS | BROKER REPORT 📊

• Brokerage: Prabhudas Lilladher

• Rating: BUY

• Target Price: ₹1,800/share

• Brokerage remains positive on Prestige Estates Projects outlook and growth prospects 🟢

UBS, AREND KΑΡΤΕΥΝ

Supply Of Chips Is Way Below Demand At The Moment

India Is Very Sensitive To Energy Disruptions Being A Net Importer

Chip Supply Shortage Will Extend Al Tech Cycle Benefitting Taiwan, Korea, China

Right Now The Market Is One Directional, Focussed On Al & Energy Prices

In The Near-term Al Is Inflationary, Expect 40 bps Increase In Inflation Due To Al

CANDACE BROWNING
BOFA GLOBAL RESEARCH

Trade off lies between war and Al capex boom

Oil prices can rebound to $85 by end of 2026 if war ends by June

GDP growth seen at 2.2% in 2026 and 2.2% in 2027

Agri sector may take a little longer to normalise

Al investment to continue for another 12 to 18 months

Better value seen in Al companies in Taiwan and Korea than in the US

Huge opportunity lies for India in the long run

More rate hikes likely by the US Fed

Inflation remains a big concern in the US

Promoter says they plan to sell 10L shares post QIP completion, which is going to take place in the next two months

Generally, promoters do not disclose, but one of the regulations of SEBI says that if you plan to sell promoter stake post a significant event, you need to take Pre Clearance Approval from SEBI under PIT Regulations

Promoters have been avoiding pre-intimating it under the guise of "earlier, there was no plan"

**LG ELECTRONICS INDIA – EXPORTS & PREMIUMISATION DRIVING GROWTH**  

**Brokerage View**
- YES Securities reiterated BUY rating
- Target price set at ₹1,776
- Implies 19.2% upside from CMP ₹1,489
- Valuation based on 45x FY28 EPS

**Growth Outlook**
- Management guiding mid-teen revenue growth for FY27
- Exports expected to become key growth driver
- Premiumisation increasing average selling prices
- Revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR estimated at 14%/25%/26% over FY26–FY28

**Export Expansion**
- Large-capacity refrigerator exports started in Q1FY27
- Presence expanding across 22 countries
- Side-by-side refrigerators gaining traction globally
- Export business delivering superior margins and FX hedge

**Home Appliances Performance**
- Segment revenue grew 5.7% YoY in Q4FY26
- Premium washing machines and refrigerators saw healthy demand
- Dishwashers emerged as a strong growth category
- EBIT margin stood at 11.9%

**Home Entertainment Strength**
- Segment revenue increased 19.6% YoY
- Large-screen television demand remained strong
- OLED TV market share maintained at 60%
- EBIT margin came at 13.4%

**Premium Product Momentum**
- Strong traction in 5-star ACs and French-door refrigerators
- Essential Series gaining market acceptance
- New product launches across premium categories underway
- Cricket World Cup boosted premium TV demand

**Localization & Manufacturing**
- Localization improved to 55.2% in FY26
- Targeting annual localization improvement of 1–2%
- Sri City plant investment of ₹50 Bn progressing
- Phased commissioning expected from late CY26

**Pricing & Margins**
- Company implemented price hikes to protect margins
- Additional price hikes planned from July onwards
- Commodity inflation and INR depreciation impacted profitability
- Margins expected to remain in low double digits

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue grew 8.1% YoY to ₹80,537 Mn
- EBITDA stood at ₹9,455 Mn
- EBITDA margin at 11.7%
- PAT came at ₹6,928 Mn

**Balance Sheet & Cash Position**
- Cash balance stood at ₹44.76 Bn
- Working capital at ₹22.73 Bn
- Debt-free balance sheet maintained
- FY26 capex stood at ₹5.5 Bn

**Future Triggers**
- Export growth expected to cushion domestic slowdown
- RAC demand improved during April and May
- Higher localization to support margins
- Sri City expansion to aid future capacity growth

Source: YES Securities

**MINDA CORPORATION – PREMIUM ELECTRONICS & EV GROWTH MOMENTUM**  

**Brokerage View**
- Axis Securities maintained BUY rating
- Target price set at ₹710
- Implies 23% upside from CMP ₹575
- Valuation based on 28x FY28E EPS

**Order Book Strength**
- Lifetime order book crossed ₹10,000 Cr
- Q4FY26 new wins worth ~₹3,500 Cr
- Around 20% orders linked to exports
- Strong visibility across 48–60 month execution cycle

**Premium & EV Expansion**
- Won lifetime sunroof order worth ~₹1,050 Cr
- Mass production expected within 4–5 months
- Expanded TFT cluster portfolio from 3-inch to 15-inch displays
- EV focus strengthened through Turntide Technologies JV

**Technology & Innovation**
- Filed 11 new patents during Q4FY26
- Total patent filings reached 330
- 147 patents already granted
- Over 950 engineers across global R&D centres

**Export & Global Growth**
- Export-led strategy gaining momentum
- Facilities in Vietnam & Indonesia supporting expansion
- Pune die-casting plant positioned as export hub
- Diversifying beyond domestic auto cycle

**Flash Electronics Performance**
- Associate Flash Electronics revenue at ~₹493 Cr
- EBITDA surged 106% YoY to ₹89 Cr
- PAT increased 137% YoY to ₹49 Cr
- Export programs and EV portfolio driving growth

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue grew 29% YoY to ₹1,704 Cr
- EBITDA increased 33% YoY to ₹203 Cr
- EBITDA margin improved to 11.9%
- PAT jumped 138% YoY to ₹124 Cr

**Margin & Operating Leverage**
- EBITDA margins guided above 11%
- Premiumisation improving profitability
- Automation & AI-driven efficiencies supporting margins
- Commodity inflation managed through pass-through mechanisms

**Capex & Capacity Expansion**
- FY26 capex stood at record ₹413 Cr
- FY27 capex guidance at ₹400–450 Cr
- New electronics and die-casting facilities underway
- Expansion focused on EV and export opportunities

**Future Outlook**
- Management targets 1.5x industry growth
- Revenue CAGR of 16–18% expected over FY26–FY28
- EBITDA & PAT CAGR projected at 22% and 30%
- Vision 2030 targets ₹17,500 Cr topline

**Company Snapshot**
- Market capitalization at ₹13,729 Cr
- FY28E Revenue estimated at ₹8,601 Cr
- FY28E PAT estimated at ₹606 Cr
- Debt-to-equity expected to improve to 0.4x

Source: Axis Securities

**Brokerage Action**
- Emkay downgraded stock to ADD from BUY
- Target Price revised to ₹2,000 from ₹2,100
- Brokerage sees upside capped near TP
- FY27 expected to be a transition year

- EBITDA grew 6% YoY to ₹3,954 Cr
- EBITDA margin declined to 27.1%
- Adjusted PAT declined 5% YoY

**Domestic Business Strength**
- India formulations business grew 15% YoY
- Growth exceeded brokerage expectations
- Market share gain of 30 bps achieved in FY26
- Strongest gain since Ranbaxy acquisition

**Specialty Portfolio**
- Global specialty sales crossed USD 1.1 Bn in FY26
- Ilumya sales reached USD 796 Mn
- Ilumya launched across 40 countries
- Non-Ilumya specialty portfolio also performing well

**New Product Momentum**
- Positive physician response for Unloxcyt
- Repeat purchases seen from cancer centers
- Early-stage traction for Leqselvi & Unloxcyt
- Psoriatic Arthritis expansion for Ilumya nearing

**US & Global Markets**
- US sales growth remained below expectations
- Specialty and generic sales softer in US
- Emerging Markets revenue grew 24% YoY
- API business revenue increased 26% YoY

**Organon Acquisition**
- Acquisition expected to close by Q4FY27
- Dedicated integration office established
- Turnaround dependent on external market factors
- Medicaid-linked pressure impacting Nexplanon sales

**Manufacturing & Capex**
- New greenfield MP facility under development
- Facility dedicated to sterile manufacturing
- Capacity aimed at global regulated markets
- Focus on US market supply expansion

**R&D & Financial Position**
- FY27 R&D spend guided at 6–7% of sales
- Specialty R&D contributed 39% of FY26 R&D
- Net cash balance stood at USD 3.2 Bn
- Strong balance sheet provides flexibility

**Financial Outlook**
- FY27 Revenue estimated at ₹64,781 Cr
- FY27 EBITDA estimated at ₹19,726 Cr
- FY27 PAT estimated at ₹13,713 Cr
- EBITDA margin expected at 30.5%

Source: Emkay Research

**Strong Q4FY26 Performance**

- Segment revenue surged 57.4% YoY
